description: Plan-mode review gate: PreToolUse blocks ExitPlanMode unless review-plan has produced a slug-scoped gate file at ~/.claude/plans/.review-ready-<slug>, or a prior successful exit left an `.exited-<slug>` sentinel (idempotent re-exit allowed silently). Active slug is identified from the session transcript (transcript_path on hook stdin), with mtime fallback only when the transcript is unavailable — avoids misidentifying the active plan when ~/.claude/plans/ holds stale files from other sessions. PostToolUse renames the gate to the sentinel and emits an advisory nudge to invoke /schedule-plan-tasks. A second PreToolUse gate (plan-unknowns-gate.py) blocks plans that lack an '## Open Unknowns' section: it has Codex (OpenAI CLI, read-only sandbox) audit the plan for unknowns that were never investigated, and instructs the planner to resolve each one (or plan a concrete resolution step) before recording the remainder — unknowns get planned, not disclaimed. Degrades to a self-audit instruction when the codex CLI is unavailable; opt out with CLAUDE_PLAN_UNKNOWNS_GATE=0.

hooks.statusMessage: Codex auditing plan for unresolved unknowns...
